steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both Cane and Billy Budd, Sailor entered the public domain within a year of each other (2019 and 2020). The sentence should emphasize this shared characteristic by grouping the two works together.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "*Cane*, a novel by Jean Toomer, entered the public domain in 2019, unlike Herman Melville's novella *Billy Budd*, Sailor, which would do so later.": Uses contrast phrasing that implies the works differ, which contradicts the goal of emphasizing their similarity. ✗
  • "*Cane*, a novel by Jean Toomer, and *Billy Budd*, Sailor, a novella by Herman Melville, recently entered the public domain.": Uses ‘and’ to group both titles, then states they ‘recently entered the public domain’ — directly emphasizing the shared trait. ✓
  • "The year was 2020, and the copyright to Herman Melville's *Billy Budd*, *Sailor* had finally expired.": Focuses on only one of the two works rather than treating them together. ✗
  • "*Cane* is a novel, whereas *Billy Budd*, Sailor is a novella.": Highlights how the works differ rather than what they share. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
